expectations.text: Block class implements build() returning a render array
expectations.evidence: Output file RelatedContentBlock.php line 52: 'public function build()' is defined and returns an array (lines 73-78) with '#theme', '#items', '#title' keys — a valid render array, but missing '#cache'.

expectations.text: Render array includes #cache key with 'tags' array
expectations.evidence: Output file RelatedContentBlock.php lines 73-78: return array contains only '#theme', '#items', '#title' — NO '#cache' key. The #cache key is entirely absent from the render array.

expectations.text: Cache tags include node-specific tags (e.g., 'node:ID' pattern for displayed nodes)
expectations.evidence: No cache tags exist anywhere in the output file. There is no getCacheTags() override and no $node->getCacheTags() call. The 'use Drupal\Core\Cache\Cache' import is absent. No node-specific tags are set.
No getCacheContexts() method exists in the baseline code. No cache contexts are set anywhere in the file. The block will serve the same cached version to all users regardless of route.
No getCacheContexts() method exists. No 'user' context is set. In production, the block's group-membership filtering will break because all users will see the same cached block output.

expectations.text: Code sets #cache metadata with both 'tags' and 'contexts' keys in the render array, AND does NOT set max-age to 0 anywhere
expectations.evidence: Output file RelatedContentBlock.php has no #cache key at all in the render array — no tags, no contexts, no max-age. The assertion requires both 'tags' and 'contexts' keys to exist, which they do not. Previously this passed vacuously with the old 'no max-age: 0' wording; tightened assertion now correctly catches the absence of cache metadata.

expectations.text: Block class implements getCacheContexts() or getCacheTags() methods, or sets cache metadata in build()
expectations.evidence: Output file RelatedContentBlock.php has no getCacheContexts() method, no getCacheTags() method, and no '#cache' key in the build() return array. None of the three acceptable paths are present.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en related_content_block returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4: module confirmed enabled via 'pm:list --status=enabled | grep related_content_block' returning '(related_content_block)'. Exit code 0.

claims.claim: The block is functionally correct (displays nodes) but has no cache metadata
claims.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php correctly queries nodes, loads them, and renders links. The functionality works; the caching is entirely absent.
The assertion passes because no cache metadata exists at all, not because the developer actively avoided max-age: 0. This is the weakest discriminating assertion in this eval.

suggestions.reason: This assertion passes vacuously in the without_skill case — the absence of max-age: 0 is because all cache metadata is absent, not because the developer made a correct choice. Consider replacing or supplementing with: 'render array uses Cache::PERMANENT or a positive integer for max-age' which would actually fail in the baseline.

eval_feedback.overall: Five of the eight failures are highly discriminating and demonstrate the skill's core value. The caching skill's 'golden rule' (EVERY render array needs #cache) is the key differentiator: without it, the baseline omits cache metadata entirely. This is the strongest eval result across all 4 skills.
